data.title: Trending stocks amid Wall Street rally this week
data.description:
U.S. stocks rallied over the trading week, shortened by the Thanksgiving holiday and a reduced trading session on Friday.

The S&P 500 was up around 4% during the week, while the Dow rose a similar amount and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite increased 5.5%.

Despite the rally, the market was still down over the course of November, weighed down by a pullback in tech stocks and reflected by a 2.5% decline in the Nasdaq Composite.

The market is bracing for lower rates after New York Fed President John Williams said there was a possibility of “a further adjustment in the near term.”

Here’s what caught investors’ attention this week:

Nvidia (NVDA) shares slipped after reports that Meta (META) is in talks to spend billions on Google’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) AI chips, signaling Google’s push to challenge Nvidia’s dominance. Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) shares, meanwhile, climbed toward the $4 trillion mark.

Tilray Brands (TLRY) fell after the cannabis company announced that it will implement a one-for-ten reverse stock split of its common stock.

Intel (INTC) shares jumped on Friday after TF International Securities analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggested the chip giant could become the foundry supplier for some of Apple's (AAPL) M-series processors.

Novo Nordisk (NVO) fell on Monday after the Danish drugmaker said a pill version of Ozempic (semaglutide) did not slow the progression of Alzheimer’s disease in two large studies that had aimed to explore a potential new use for its blockbuster obesity drug. As a result, Novo Nordisk will halt plans for a one-year extension of the trials based on the efficacy results observed in the overall study population.

Zscaler (ZS) shares fell on Wednesday even though the company beat earnings and revenue estimates and issued guidance slightly above expectations. The company posted adjusted EPS of $0.96 on $788.1M in revenue, both ahead of forecasts, and guided Q2 revenue and earnings above consensus.

Tesla (TSLA) shares jumped on Monday after Elon Musk said the company is close to completing its AI5 chips and has already started early work on AI6. He added that Tesla vehicles currently use AI4 chips and that the coming generations could “profoundly change the world,” enabling safer driving and expanded access to advanced medical care through the Optimus platform.

Shares in NYSE owner Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) and Nasdaq (NDAQ) were in focus after derivatives exchange operator CME Group (CME) halted trading across its markets on Friday, due to a cooling issue at CyrusOne data centers. The hours-long outage crippled key parts of global financial markets.

Zoom Communications (ZM) shares gained on Tuesday after the company reported Q3 results and guidance above expectations and unveiled a new $1B buyback. For the period ending Oct. 31, Zoom earned an adjusted $1.52 per share as revenue rose 4.4% year-over-year to come in at $1.23B. For the full-year, the company expects revenue between $4.852B and $4.857B and adjusted earnings between $5.95 and $5.97 per share.

Nutanix (NTNX) fell after the company issued weak guidance, projecting FQ2 revenue of $705M–$715M, below the $749M consensus, and cutting its full-year revenue outlook to $2.82B–$2.86B. The CFO said demand is still solid, but a revenue shift from FQ1 into later periods led to the reduced forecast, even as the company raised its full-year free cash flow guidance.

CleanSpark (CLSK) traded higher after the bitcoin miner and AI infrastructure firm posted a profit for fiscal 2025. GAAP EPS came in at $1.12, below the $1.56 consensus but up sharply from losses of $0.69 in 2024 and $1.30 in 2023. Revenue rose to $766.3M, short of expectations but roughly double last year’s total and well above 2023 levels.

data.title: American Air says one aircraft left to be fixed of 209 total impacted by Airbus issue
data.description:
American Airlines (AAL), the world's largest operator of the Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF)(OTCPK:EADSY) A320, on Saturday said only one of its aircraft was left to be fixed of the 209 total impacted by a sweeping recall of the jet by the European planemaker.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF)(OTCPK:EADSY), the top global planemaker, on Friday said about 6,000 of its A320 jets across the world would need an immediate software fix, a move that threatened to disrupt flights globally and during the busiest travel weekend of the year in the U.S.

American Airlines (AAL) on Friday had said the software issue would affect about 340 of its aircraft. Saturday's update reduced that number.  

"As of 9 a.m. CT, just one aircraft remains to be updated of the 209 impacted, and work on that aircraft will begin in the next two hours," AAL told Seeking Alpha in an emailed statement. 

"American expects no further operational impact related to the Emergency Airworthiness Directive and looks forward to the remaining days of the Thanksgiving holiday travel period, especially Sunday, our most-traveled day," the airline added.

data.title: Real estate stocks extend gains during Thanksgiving week as rate cut odds surge
data.description:
Real estate stocks rose in the Thanksgiving week as the odds of another Federal Reserve interest rate cut in December surged.

On Friday, November 21, New York Fed President John Williams said there is "room" for more monetary policy easing, sending real estate stocks higher.

According to the CME FedWatch tool, the probability of another 25 basis point rate cut in December now stands at 86.4%. The next FOMC meeting is scheduled for December 9-10.

The S&P 500 Real Estate Index Sector (SP500-60) rose 1.83% during the holiday-shortened week to close at 262.34, while its accompanying Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLRE) was up 1.88% to 41.67.

The Dow Jones REIT Indx Equity REIT Total Return Index (REIT:IND) advanced 1.76%, while the FTSE Nareit All Equity REITs index gained 1.73%.

Company News

  • Four Corners Property Trust (FCPT) will acquire two Hawaiian Bros properties for $5.9M from Stine Enterprises.
  • Sixth Street Partners said it's abandoning its previously announced non-binding proposal to acquire Plymouth Industrial REIT (PLYM).
  • Orion Properties (ONL) said the notice submitted by The Kawa Fund Limited, which purported to nominate five candidates for Orion's board, is invalid. The board unanimously determined that the submission didn't meet several requirements in Orion's bylaws, including that Kawa be a record holder of the company's common stock.
  • FrontView REIT's (FVR) board approved a stock repurchase program authorizing up to $75M in buybacks.
  • Gaming and Leisure Properties (GLPI) declared $0.78/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous. Armada Hoffler Properties (AHH) declared $0.14/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Elme Communities (ELME) declared a $14.67/share special liquidating distribution.

Winners & Losers

Among the S&P 500 real estate stocks, Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) was the best-performing stock of the week, despite being in the bottom 10 performers year-to-date.

The life sciences REIT faces an investigation from a national shareholder rights law firm over possible violations of the federal securities laws.

Nevertheless, the stock added 8.20% to close the holiday-shortened week at $53.67.

CBRE Group (CBRE) followed, with the real estate services provider gaining 6.86% week-over-week to end at $161.83.

Equinix (EQIX) (-1.92% W/W to $753.31) was the worst-performing stock of the week in the category.

Equinix, along with Digital Realty Trust (DLR), is said to be among the bidders for the Nordic data center operator atNorth. The data center REIT announced on Monday an issuance of C$700M senior notes due 2032.

For the broader real estate sector, Rocket Companies (RKT) (+17.64% W/W to $19.98), Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) (+16.29% W/W to $7.70), and Fathom Holdings (FTHM) (+14.68% W/W to $1.29) were the top gainers.

LandBridge (LB) (-12.11% W/W to $56.95) and Ohmyhome (OMH) (-11.17% W/W to $0.85) bottomed out.

Here is a look at the subsector performances:

data.title: FDA official Prasad says COVID shot deaths mean stricter vaccine approval standards
data.description:
Vinay Prasad, the head of the US FDA's Center for Biologics Evaluations and Research which oversees vaccines, said that future vaccine approvals will face stricter standards in light of the deaths of children after they received the COVID-19 shot.

In an email to CBER staff obtained by The Washington Post, Prasad said that at least 10 children died "after and because of receiving COVID-19 vaccination," adding that the deaths "are related to vaccination."

Prasad cited data from the agency's Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System that indicated that young and healthy men and boys who were the least likely to have COVID complications had a higher risk of vaccine-induced myocarditis, a serious inflammation of the heart muscle.

In June, the FDA mandated that mRNA-based COVID vaccines form Moderna (MRNA) and Pfizer (PFE) update their labeling about an increased risk of myocarditis and pericarditis following vaccination. Though not an mRNA shot, Novavax's (NVAX) COVID jab also contains the warning.

In a July JAMA article, Prasad and FDA Commissioner Marty Makary noted that the "estimated unadjusted incidence of myocarditis and/or pericarditis was approximately 27 cases per 1,000,000 (or 1 in 37 000) in males aged 12 to 24 years for the 2023 to 2024 formula of mRNA COVID-19 vaccines."

"FDA has never requested the manufacturers demonstrate in randomized fashion that vaccinating children improves" COVID outcomes, Prasad wrote in the email, adding, "We do not know how many fewer kids would have died had they been vaccinated, and we do not know how many more kids died from taking vaccines than has been voluntarily reported....it is horrifying to consider that the US vaccine regulation, including our actions, may have harmed more children than we saved."

As a result, Prasad unveiled a new "path forward" centered on "evidence based medicine" that includes denying approval for vaccines in pregnant women "based on unproven surrogate endpoints"; pneumonia vaccines will need to demonstrate a reduction in pneumonia rather than just generating antibody titers; and immunogenicity can't be used to expand a vaccine's indicated population -- these groups will need to be included in trials during development.

He added that annual flu vaccines approvals will be revised, as the current system "is an evidence-based catastrophe of low quality evidence, poor surrogate assays, and uncertain vaccine effectiveness measured in case-control studies with poor methods."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Luxury spending shines on Black Friday
data.description:
Black Friday sales in the U.S. outperformed expectations, with a 6% year-over-year jump, according to data from Salesforce. Notably, the top-performing U.S. shopping categories were luxury apparel and accessories, which saw a 21% increase in sales growth compared to a year ago.

Tapestry (TPR), Capri Holdings (CPRI), Ralph Lauren (RL), PVH Corporation (PVH), Canada Goose (GOOS), and Signet Jewelers (SIG) are a few of the consumer stocks that could get a boost if the strong luxury spending trend continues. Higher prices were a significant factor in the sales growth.

So far, U.S. luxury sales in 2025 have shown resilience amid economic uncertainty, with overall market growth projected at around 6% year-over-year, driven by affluent consumers' focus on experiences and digital channels, though traditional goods like apparel and leather have faced softness from price sensitivity and trading-down trends. Luxury retail visitation declined about 4% year-over-year through much of 2025, reflecting a pullback from aspirational buyers amid high prices and inflation.

Luxury-related ETFs include the KraneShares Global Luxury Index ETF (KLXY).

data.title: Insider trades: Boeing, Walmart, Block among notable names this week
data.description:
Boeing (BA), Walmart (WMT) and Block (XYZ) were among the companies that witnessed insider trades this week. The following is a list of transactions that occurred between November 24 and November 28.

  • Boeing’s (BA) chief information digital officer, Dana Deasy, bought 554 shares of the company at an average price of $178.89 per share, totaling $ 99,102. Following the transaction, Deasy now holds 28,442 Boeing shares.
  • Frederik Mohn, director at Transocean (RIG), purchased 1.5 million shares at $4.02 each, for a total value of $6.03M. Mohn now owns 96.60 million Transocean shares.
  • Innovative Industrial Properties’ (IIPR) Executive Chairman Alan Gold acquired 2,600 shares at an average price of $49.09, amounting to $127,634. Gold now holds 274,408 IIPR shares.
  • Clorox’s (CLX) Director Pierre Breber bought company shares worth $416,516. He purchased 4,000 shares at $104.13 each, raising his holding to 13,000 shares.
  • Walmart’s (WMT) Senior Vice President David Chojnowski sold 7,000 shares at an average price of $106.65 per share, totaling $746,550. Following the transaction, Chojnowski now holds 136,289 Walmart shares.
  • Block’s (XYZ) Engineering Lead Arnaud Weber sold 7,095 shares at $61.40 each, for a total value of $435,633. Weber now owns 259,136 shares of Block.
  • Applied Materials’ (AMAT) Chief Legal Officer Teri Little sold 4,000 shares at an average price of $238.24 per share, worth about $952,942. Little now holds 84,351 AMAT shares.
  • Eric Shroff, senior vice president at Abbott Laboratories (ABT), let go of 1,586 shares at $128.02 each, for a total value of $ 203,043. Shroff now owns 30,384 Abbott shares.
  • Unity Software’s (U) CEO and President Matthew Bromberg divested 41,025 shares at an average price of $41.38, totaling $1.70 million. After the sale, Bromberg continues to hold 1.19M Unity shares.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Baidu, Shell and T-Applied Materials among top picks
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) closed in the green on Friday, while Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) rose 1.5% and 2.1%, respectively for the week.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Baidu gets bullish views from JPM on cloud, AI growth

J.P. Morgan upgraded Chinese internet giant Baidu (BIDU) to Overweight from Neutral and said cloud and AI are set to become the primary growth engines and value drivers for the company, rather than a rebound in its legacy advertising.

The brokerage believes revenue growth from AI and cloud will be driven by Kunlun chip sales and GPU compute consumption.

"We view Kunlun AI chip as one of the best positioned. We forecast chip sales to increase sixfold, reaching RMB 8B in 2026," according to JPM analyst Alex Yao. "We believe domestic demand for AI compute in China remains intense, and hyperscalers are increasingly sourcing from local solution providers."

Shell downgraded to Neutral at UBS

UBS downgraded Shell (SHEL) to Neutral from Buy, saying the stock no longer screens as cheap on multiples after rising 12% YTD, led by a series of cash flow beats helped by lower-than-expected operating costs.

UBS analyst Joshua Stone said he continues to see Shell as having an important role to play in any energy portfolio given its strong financial footing and hard-to-replace business in Mobility and Integrated Gas. However, he said the medium-term challenges associated with resource replenishment and more limited top-line growth are becoming harder to justify at the stock's current valuation.

Applied Materials upgraded to Buy on memory strength

Applied Materials (AMAT) was upgraded by UBS to Buy, citing the recent strength in the memory business.

The brokerage also raised PT to $285 from $250, reflecting a “significantly more bullish outlook for wafer fab equipment in C2026/2027E.”

UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri wrote in a note to clients. “Our revised forecast calls for 2026 [wafer fab equipment] to increase more than 20% Y/Y to $136.5B, driven primarily by memory. We now expect DRAM [wafer fab equipment] to rise by ~$13B Y/Y (vs. our prior [estimates] of ~$9B), accounting for more than half of the anticipated ~$25B increase in total [wafer fab equipment]. Looking ahead to 2027, we believe [wafer fab equipment] approaches $145B, a level well above current Street expectations.”

Morgan Stanley moved to a restricted rating from equal weight on Kimberly-Clark (KMB). It wasn't immediately clear why the rating was changed to restricted. There's a chance that Kimberly-Clark is a potential acquisition target or it's also possible that Morgan Stanley was hired for activist defense, especially after the planned $40 billion purchase of Kenvue, traders told Seeking. Alpha.

Mizuho also raised the price target on Lumentum's (LITE) stock to $325 from $290, noting that Google’s Tensor Processing Units, or TPUs, ramp-up is positive for Lumentum.

HSBC has initiated coverage of Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) at Buy, citing the potential for increased Eylea HD (aflibercept) sales next year as well as upcoming catalysts for some of its cancer candidates in the pipeline. The bank has a target price of $890 on the stock. HSBC projects 2026 adjusted diluted EPS of $43.48 for Regeneron compared to the consensus of $42.98.

Wedbush Securities double upgraded Fannie Mae (FNMA) to Outperform from Underperform and increased PT to $11.50 from $1.00. "Our rating, price target and investment outlook are based on our analysis of the implications of a range of steps that the Trump Administration might take to recapitalize FNMA, put it on the path to leaving conservatorship, and monetize Treasury's investments in the company," said analyst Henry Coffey.

data.title: Crypto-tied stocks surge, insurers slip: weekly financials wrap
data.description:
Financial stocks largely climbed with the broader equity market in the past five trading sessions, led by crypto-tied firms, as bitcoin (BTC-USD) rebounded during the holiday-shortened week.

CleanSpark (CLSK) surged 54% in the past five sessions ended Nov. 28, 2025, after the bitcoin (BTC-USD) miner and AI infrastructure provider turned in a profit for fiscal-year 2025, albeit softer than expected;

Cipher Mining (CIFR), which during the week was upgraded along with CLSK at J.P. Morgan, gapped up 40%;

TeraWulf (WULF) spiked 34%;

Bitfarms (BITF) rose 34%; and

Hut 8 (HUT) gained 33%.

On the negative side, American Bitcoin (ABTC) shed 7.4%;

RLI (RLI) edged down 3.8%;

Primerica (PRI) was off 1.6%;

Freedom Holding (FRHC) slipped 1%; and

Unum Group (UNM) fell 0.4%.

data.title: Micron said to invest $9.6B in AI memory chip production facility in Japan
data.description:
U.S. memory chipmaker Micron Technology (MU) will invest 1.5T yen ($9.6B) to build a production facility in western Japan for chips to power AI computing, Nikkei Asia reported on Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Micron's (MU) decision is aimed at diversifying its advanced chip production away from a concentration in Taiwan, Nikkei said. The new factory will make next-generation high-bandwidth memory chips, a core component of AI chips.

Micron (MU) will start building the new factory in May 2026, Nikkei said, with a plan to launch shipments of the high-bandwidth memory chips around 2028. Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry will subsidize the project to the tune of up to 500B yen, Nikkei added.

Micron (MU) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Trump says Venezuela airspace 'to be closed in its entirety' as tensions escalate
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Saturday said the airspace above and surrounding Venezuela should be considered "to be closed in its entirety," amid escalating tensions between the two countries.

"To all Airlines, Pilots, Drug Dealers, and Human Traffickers, please consider THE AIRSPACE ABOVE AND SURROUNDING VENEZUELA TO BE CLOSED IN ITS ENTIRETY," the president posted on his Truth Social service.

The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration last Saturday had warned operators of civilian aircraft to exercise caution when flying in and around Venezuelan airspace. 

Relations between the U.S. and Venezuela have been deteriorating for months, with Trump accusing the South American nation's leader, Nicolas Maduro, of helping to supply illegal drugs to the U.S. Maduro has denied the allegations and says Trump seeks to oust him. Meanwhile, U.S. forces have continued to build up in and around Venezuela and have conducted multiple strikes on vessels allegedly trafficking drugs in the Caribbean Sea.

Airline indexes and stocks: Dow Jones U.S. Airlines Index (DJUSAR), American Airlines (AAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Southwest Airlines (LUV), United Airlines (UAL), JetBlue (JBLU), Hawaiian Holdings (HA), Alaska Air (ALK), Allegiant Travel (ALGT), Spirit Airlines (SAVE), Mesa Airlines (MESA), SkyWest (SKYW), Sun Country Airlines (SNCY), and Frontier Group (ULCC).
